title: The Work of the Holy Spirit
intro: The Spirit has been working since before the first word of Scripture was written. He hovered over the waters at creation. He carried the prophets. He descended on Jesus at His baptism. And now He works in and through every believer — in creation, in salvation, in sanctification, and in witness.
reading:
Gavin's outline organizes the Spirit's work into three areas: creation and revelation, salvation, and sanctification.

Genesis 1:2 places the Spirit at the very beginning — hovering over the face of the waters before anything had yet been spoken into existence. 2 Peter 1:21 tells us that men spoke from God as they were carried along by the Holy Spirit. Every word of Scripture is the Spirit's work.

In salvation, the Spirit is not optional. John 3:5-6 declares that unless one is born of water and the Spirit, he cannot enter the kingdom of God. 1 Corinthians 12:3 says no one can even confess that Jesus is Lord except in the Holy Spirit. You did not come to faith on your own — the Spirit drew you.

In sanctification, Galatians 5:22-23 lists the fruit the Spirit produces in a yielded life: love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, and self-control. Romans 8:13-14 tells us that by the Spirit we put to death the deeds of the body and live. 2 Corinthians 3:18 says we are being transformed by the Spirit into the image of Christ — from one degree of glory to another.

And John 16:13 summarizes His ongoing mission: He will guide you into all the truth. He glorifies Christ. He illuminates the Word. He is not passive in your life — He is actively working for your transformation.
reflection: In which area of the Spirit's work — salvation, sanctification, or witness — do you feel most aware of His activity? Where do you feel most resistant or disconnected?
abide: Holy Spirit, I yield to Your work in me today — in the deep places where I still need transformation, in the moments when I am called to witness, and in the truth I need You to illuminate. Do what only You can do.
